title: Raycast basics
goal: Cast a downward ray from an emitter part when something touches it, and print what it hit.

assets.name: Emitter rig
assets.description: Pre-built target rig used as the ray's hit surface. Bundled in TutorialsPlace.rbxlx.

steps.body:
Raycasting is how code asks the world "what's along this line?" —
useful for lasers, line-of-sight checks, projectile previews, or
just "what did the player step on?" The modern API is
`workspace:Raycast(origin, direction)`. You pass a starting point
and a direction vector (length = max distance), and the engine
returns a result with `.Instance`, `.Position`, `.Normal`, and
`.Material` — or `nil` if nothing was in the way.

We'll build a small demo: a flat emitter part that fires a
downward ray whenever something touches it, and a target rig
sitting below it so the ray has something to report.

A flat, anchored Part in Workspace acts as our emitter. Flat so the
touch surface is obvious, anchored so physics doesn't drag it
around while you're testing.

A server Script under the emitter connects to its `.Touched`
event and casts a ray 50 studs straight down. `Vector3.yAxis`
reads cleaner than `Vector3.new(0, 1, 0)` and avoids typos.

When the ray hits something, we read the hit instance and
position out of the result and print them. When it misses,
`result` is `nil` and we say so.
steps.source:
-- Fires a downward ray from the emitter each time it's touched.
local emitter = script.Parent

local RAY_LENGTH = 50

emitter.Touched:Connect(function(_other)
	local origin = emitter.Position
	local direction = -Vector3.yAxis * RAY_LENGTH
	local result = workspace:Raycast(origin, direction)

	if result then
		print(`Ray hit: {result.Instance:GetFullName()} at {result.Position}`)
	else
		print("Ray missed — nothing in range.")
	end
end)

steps.body:
For the ray to hit something interesting, drop the pre-built
target rig into Workspace. It's bundled in the shared tutorials
place under `ReplicatedStorage.TutorialAssets`, so no marketplace
lookup or external download is needed.

Press ▶ Play (or F5). Walk your character onto the cyan emitter
— each step triggers a fresh raycast and you'll see
"Ray hit: Workspace.Target.*" lines stream into the Output
window. Step off the target and the ray will eventually hit the
baseplate or report a miss. Shift+F5 to stop.
